title: How to be social in College - Request

transcript.text: Video Request. Can you give some tips on how to be more social and gain new friends in a place such as college?
The first semester at MCC kind of sucked for me as I was very quiet and hardly talked much.
And when I do talk, my shyness ends up making my voice sounding sort of weak.
I want to know what I could do on making the next semester at MCC much better than the first.
Alright, well, if you're in college, you should be doing the socialization.
That's probably the main reason people go to college.
You know, if you want to learn stuff, you're better off just studying yourself and not paying tuition.
So take advantage of what college has to offer, which is all the social stuff.
Colleges have all of these clubs and stuff.
So just join a club.
I was a little bit, I kind of know where you're coming from.
Because when I first went to college, I felt kind of awkward because there'd be people always partying in other people's dorm rooms.
And I never got the point of why you would just show up at someone's dorm room just to like hang out just for the sake of hang out and drink just for the sake of drinking, you know.
Because it kind of means that now you've got like a clique, you know, and that you've just defined you're part of some little clique.
And I don't like cliques, you know, small cliques and stuff.
So, yeah, being part of like a club or a sport team or something, as long as you get along with the people in it, you know.
Just find one of those where you click with the people and then when you go and hang out with the people, you're killing two birds with one stone.
You're hanging out with them to serve a purpose, you know, to do some kind of social betterment activity, you know.
You're the Amnesty International Club or something, debate club or something.
So you're, and you're also making friends and having actual relationships with people, you know.
Like actual conversations with people, two-way conversations that are engaging and something that a college is supposed to be about.
You know, you're not just getting drunk and talking trash about other people and like being stupid, you know.
So, and there's all sorts of that stuff.
I don't know what BCC is or wherever, what place are you talking about?
MCC, I don't know where that is, but community college.
Even a little community college has got tons of clubs and sports.
So, just join one of those and you, you're, you're off, you're, you're, you're, you're shooting for the stars.
You're all squirred away.

Uh, yeah, if you're quiet and you don't talk much, that's probably, that could be because you just, you know, you're not the kind of person who just likes to mow over people and talk over people and just be the center of attention or whatever.
You want to talk to say something actual, the word meaning.
So, if you go to a club or whatever that has, where people are talking about actual things, then just, when you show up for the club, make sure you have something of value for everybody to know.
And then share it, share that, and there, there you are talking.
You know?
You know, I mean, if you want to talk more, then have something to say.
You know, and if you want to have something to say, then study up a little bit and find out what you're interested in and learn about it.
And then share that with the appropriate people.
Uh, I want to know what I can do on making the next semester at MCC much better than the first.
Yeah, that's how you do it.
That's how you do it.
But, now I don't know what your situation in college is.
If your parents are putting you through college, then yeah, that's fine.
But if you're putting yourself through college, maybe you should just find out what job you want to get and get good at it.
And, uh, because a lot of people go to college and then they find out later that it was, it didn't, didn't, didn't do anything for them.
You know?
Like me.
I wasted all, all this time in college and ended up getting kicked out of the education department after I did all the classes and tests and stuff.
And now I have a, just a job where you don't even need a college degree and I'll never have to have a job that needs any kind of college degree.
So, so you might want to double, uh, think twice about why you're there in the first place.
But yeah, if you want to be more social, college is a perfect place to do that.
I think that's the number one reason that people should go to college, you know, and, and classes should be set up to allow everybody to talk, but they're not.
So, so do the clubs and even sports teams.
You talk because you're always, you're, you're naturally with the people anyway.
Brian, you say you're, then you start with the small talk and then, uh, and, uh, you know, you'll eventually start, uh, being sociable and all that.
So have fun, Brian.
